title: Hieracium flaccicaule
taxonomy: Hieracium flaccicaule belongs to the family Asteraceae and is a species of hawkweed. It is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe. The classification of Hieracium flaccicaule is further divided into the genus Hieracium and the species H. flaccicaule. The genus Hieracium is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and genetic relationships.

intro: Hieracium flaccicaule, commonly known as yellow hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, yellow hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium flaccicaule include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Yellow hawkweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds.
